metadata.title: SYNGO_CC_1721
metadata.comment:
Rationale: Fig 1 BAI1 is expressed in neurons and localizes to excitatory synapses.
1b Literal "We posited that BAI1 might be synaptogenic because of its presence in dendritic spines, apparent even in a low-magnification image
of a mature hippocampal neuron"
1d Literal "Comparing the gradient of BAI1 staining from spine heads to immediately adjacent segments of dendritic shaft revealed that BAI1 was enriched in spine heads to an extent similar to PSD95, whereas acetylated tubulin was enriched in dendritic shafts and EGFP filled both regions equally.
1e Literal "Strikingly, even overexpressed BAI1 targeted strongly to spines
1f Literal "First, we biochemically purified synapses and found that BAI1 was enriched in the PSD fraction, although to a lower extent than PSD95
1g Literal "Second, we stained mature cultured
neurons for BAI1 and the synaptic markers PSD95 or GluR1 (Fig.
1G). In both cases, BAI1 colocalized significantly with these markers in spines, indicative of synaptic localization."

9/11/2017 Pim
- Based on the known transmembrane topology of the protein, the "integral component of ... membrane" term was chosen.
- Postsynaptic localization is directly shown in the paper. Term 'integral component of postsynaptic membrane (GO:0099055)' was inferred from TM domains and known rol in cell adhesion.. Experimental description: Experiments were done in rats hippocampus neurons
Literal "Hippocampal neurons were cultured from E19 Long-Evans rats as described
previously."

9/11/2017 Pim
- Overexpressed BAI1 protein is of human species; "Human BAI1 was obtained from Y. Nakamura (University of Tokyo) ". Since endogenous rat protein was also visualized annotation is made against rat species.
